name: Joe Chambers
description: American jazz drummer, pianist, vibraphonist and composer
abstract: Joe Chambers (born June 25, 1942) is an American jazz drummer, pianist, vibraphonist and composer. In the 1960s and 1970s, Chambers gigged with many high-profile artists such as Eric Dolphy, Charles Mingus, Wayne Shorter, and Chick Corea and played on several iconic Blue Note albums of the 1960s.

concerts.label: New York Jazz Repertory Company: Jazz With a Spanish Tinge

about_quotes.speaker: Jason Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: Absolutely I know about Joe Chambers. I know about Joe from his Blue Note recordings...
about_quotes.doc_title: Jason Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Chick Corea
about_quotes.evidence: One of the first bands I put together that I was real excited about was the band that finally made my first recording, Tones For Joan’s Bones, with Steve Swallow, Joe Farrell, Joe Chambers, and Woody Shaw.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chick Corea — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Hutcherson
about_quotes.evidence: We did that Now album. ... I always thought that was really different, because that was the first time I did a thing with voices... Joe's writing like mad. We're doing all this writing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Hutcherson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: Joe Chambers was on drums... Yeah, Joe Chambers, Ron, and Chick were the rhythm section for Shades of Light and Windows.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Wall and Adam Nussbaum
about_quotes.evidence: Joe Chambers is mentioned exclusively by the [INTERVIEWER] as an example of a musician the interviewer worked with; the [INTERVIEWEE] does not mention Joe Chambers or indicate any personal interaction.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Wall and Adam Nussbaum interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 19, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Chuck Israels
about_quotes.evidence: Bill auditioned a drummer I thought was, named Joe Chambers, and I thought this guy’s going to be good. But Bill thought he was not yet developed enough.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chuck Israels interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 11, 2021
